subject: AP Macroeconomics
classification_standard: A student who has completed ONLY this unit should be able to answer this question.

units.name: Basic Economic Concepts
distinguishing_features: No macroeconomic aggregates (GDP, unemployment, inflation)
No government policy (fiscal or monetary)
No money/banking concepts
Supply and demand analysis is micro-level (individual markets, not economy-wide)
boundary_rules: Market for a specific product (wheat, bottled water, cars) = U1
Equilibrium in a specific product market = U1 (NOT AD-AS equilibrium)
Comparative advantage calculation = U1
PPF analysis = U1
example_questions: Supply and demand for a specific product
Effects of price changes in a single market

units.name: Economic Indicators and the Business Cycle
distinguishing_features: Measurement and definition of macro indicators
No policy analysis (how to fix unemployment/inflation)
No market models (AD-AS, money market, loanable funds)
Identification and calculation of GDP, unemployment, inflation
boundary_rules: Types of unemployment = U2
Causes of inflation (cost-push vs demand-pull) = U2 (DEFINITIONAL, not AD-AS analysis)
GDP calculation = U2
Real vs nominal = U2
IF the question shows AD-AS graph to explain inflation → U3 (not U2)
IF the question asks 'how to reduce inflation' using policy → U3, U4, or U5
example_questions: Calculate inflation rate from CPI data

units.name: National Income and Price Determination
excluded_concepts: interest rate determination by money market
distinguishing_features: AD-AS is the CORE MODEL
Fiscal policy only (government spending, taxes, multipliers)
Short-run focus (SRAS, SRPC, short-run gaps)
Monetary policy terms may appear as DISTRACTORS only
boundary_rules: AD-AS equilibrium with LRAS = U3 (taught in U3 as AD-AS model)
LRAS shifting OUTWARD for growth = U5 (growth is U5)
Fiscal policy affecting AD = U3
Fiscal policy + monetary policy (explicit combination) = U5
Monetary policy terms in WRONG options only = U3 (distractors)
SRPC = U3 (short-run Phillips curve taught in U3)
LRPC = U5 (long-run Phillips curve taught in U5)
Crowding out = U5 (explicitly taught in U5)
Inflation explained WITH AD-AS model = U3 (not U2)
Inflation explained with definitions only = U2
example_questions: AD shifts left due to decrease in consumer spending
Government spending increase with multiplier effect
SRPC trade-off between inflation and unemployment

units.name: Financial Sector
distinguishing_features: Money and banking focus
Monetary policy tools (OMO, discount rate, reserve requirement)
Interest rate determination (money market, loanable funds)
NO fiscal policy combinations
boundary_rules: Central bank buys/sells bonds = U4 (open market operations)
Money supply changes = U4
Money demand shifts = U4
Loanable funds market = U4
Monetary policy affecting AD = U4 (if testing monetary transmission mechanism)
Monetary policy as AD shifter in AD-AS graph = U3 or U4 (context-dependent)
Fiscal policy in background + loanable funds in foreground = U4
Interest rate changes from monetary policy = U4
example_questions: Open market purchase effect on money supply

units.name: Long-Run Consequences of Stabilization Policies
distinguishing_features: Policy COMBINATIONS (fiscal + monetary together)
Long-run focus (growth, LRPC, crowding out)
Growth theory (production function, human capital, technology)
boundary_rules: EXPLICIT 'which combination of fiscal and monetary policy' → U5
Government spending + central bank buys/sells bonds → U5
Taxes + open market operations → U5
Any question with BOTH fiscal action AND monetary action → U5
Long-run growth with PPF/LRAS outward shift → U5
Crowding out → U5
LRPC → U5
Debt-financed government spending → U5 (crowding out context)
example_questions: Effect of human capital on long-run growth

units.name: Open Economy—International Trade and Finance
distinguishing_features: Foreign trade and finance
Exchange rates and foreign exchange market
International capital flows
boundary_rules: Exchange rate effect on trade = U6
Balance of payments = U6
Tariff/quotas effect = U6
Monetary policy affecting exchange rate = U6 (if testing exchange rate)
Monetary policy in domestic context = U4
example_questions: Exchange rate appreciation effect on exports

cross_unit_rules.name: Highest unit number rule (most important)
cross_unit_rules.rule: If a question involves concepts from multiple units, ALWAYS classify it to the HIGHEST unit number. A student must have learned all involved units to answer this question, so it belongs to the last unit learned. Examples: U3+U4→U4, U4+U6→U6, U3+U5→U5, U1+U3+U6→U6.

cross_unit_rules.name: Micro vs Macro equilibrium
cross_unit_rules.rule: If the question is about a SPECIFIC PRODUCT MARKET (wheat, bottled water, cars) → U1. If the question is about the WHOLE ECONOMY (AD-AS, national output) → U3.

cross_unit_rules.name: Inflation definitional vs analytical
cross_unit_rules.rule: If the question asks WHAT inflation is, its types, or how to measure it → U2. If the question uses AD-AS to EXPLAIN inflation or asks how policy affects inflation → U3, U4, or U5.

cross_unit_rules.name: Monetary policy alone vs combination
cross_unit_rules.rule: Central bank action ALONE (buying/selling bonds, changing reserve requirement) → U4. Central bank action + government fiscal action → U5.

cross_unit_rules.name: Fiscal policy alone vs combination
cross_unit_rules.rule: Government spending/taxes ALONE (multiplier, AD shift) → U3. Government spending/taxes + monetary policy → U5.

cross_unit_rules.name: LRAS in different contexts
cross_unit_rules.rule: LRAS in AD-AS equilibrium model → U3. LRAS shifting outward with growth factors → U5.

cross_unit_rules.name: Phillips curve SR vs LR
cross_unit_rules.rule: Short-run Phillips curve (trade-off) → U3. Long-run Phillips curve (vertical) → U5.

cross_unit_rules.name: Interest rate context
cross_unit_rules.rule: Interest rate from money market/loanable funds → U4. Interest rate as side effect of fiscal policy in AD-AS → U3.

cross_unit_rules.name: Distractor detection
cross_unit_rules.rule: If a concept from another unit appears ONLY in wrong answer options, it is a DISTRACTOR and does not change the primary unit.

common_pitfalls: NEVER classify by keyword frequency alone
NEVER classify by first keyword mentioned
NEVER let distractors determine the unit
ALWAYS distinguish micro equilibrium (U1) from macro equilibrium (U3)
ALWAYS check: would a student who only studied this unit know this concept?
For cross-unit questions: ALWAYS take the HIGHEST unit number, never the core concept unit
Do NOT ignore cross-unit synthesis: any question with U6 concepts belongs to U6, even if it also has U3 concepts
